sanskrit:
अपरेयमितस्त्वन्यां प्रकृतिं विद्धि मे पराम्।
जीवभूतां महाबाहो ययेदं धार्यते जगत्॥
transliteration:
apareyam itas tv anyāṃ prakṛtiṃ viddhi me parām |
jīva-bhūtāṃ mahā-bāho yayedaṃ dhāryate jagat ||
literal_meaning: Besides this inferior [material] energy, know another, higher nature of Mine, O mighty-armed Arjuna—the living beings—by which this universe is sustained.
contextual_meaning: Krishna distinguishes two layers of reality: the inert, observable world (inferior prakṛti) and the sentient, experiencing self (superior prakṛti). Psychologically, this is the difference between external objects/events and the internal observer who gives them meaning. Health requires aligning both layers under the Supreme rather than letting the ego identify solely with either.
psychological_state: Cognitive dissonance resolving into expanded self-concept; shift from object-focused to observer-focused awareness.
trigger_scenario: A burnout patient realizes they are not their job title, bank balance, or diagnostic label, but the conscious agent capable of re-writing the narrative.
anchor_text: You are the conscious witness within, not the passing world outside.
